login.title: Sign in

errors.missingSalt: No "salt" found on the user profile.
errors.invalidEncryptedKey: Invalid encrypted key on the user profile. Please sign in again.
errors.missingEncryptedKey: Missing encrypted key on the user profile. Please sign in again.
errors.decryptFailure: Unable to decrypt the encryption key. Please sign in again.
errors.corruptedKey: Corrupted encryption key. Please sign in again.

login.sessionLostBanner: You've been signed out. Please sign in again.
login.privacyLink: See how Nodea protects my data
login.activatedBanner: Account activated. Sign in with your email and password.
login.passwordChangedBanner: Password updated. Sign in with the new one.
login.passkeyNoDecrypt: This passkey can't decrypt your data. Enter your password to finish.

errors.invalidInvite: Invalid or already used invitation code

closed.title: By invitation
closed.subtitle: Sign-up on this Nodea instance is by invitation only. Ask an admin to send you an invitation link by email.

invalidInvite.title: Invalid invitation link
invalidInvite.subtitle: This invitation link is not valid. It may have already been used, expired, or been truncated while copying.
invalidInvite.askNewLink: Ask an admin to send you a new link.

checkEmail.title: Check your inbox
checkEmail.subtitleBefore: We sent an activation link to
checkEmail.subtitleAfter: . Click the link to activate your account — you can sign in afterwards.
checkEmail.linkValidity: The link is valid for 7 days. Remember to check your spam folder if you can't find it right away.

created.title: Account created!
created.subtitleAfter: is ready. Redirecting you to sign-in.

form.title: Create an account
form.emailLockedHint: Email set when the invitation was issued, not editable.
form.usernameInvalid: Letters, digits, and . _ - only (2 to 32 characters).
form.usernameHint: A first name or a nickname, whichever you prefer.
form.passwordMismatch: The two passwords do not match.
form.passwordRulesUnmet: The password does not meet all the rules.

errors.passwordMismatch: The new passwords do not match.
errors.missingEncryptedKey: Main key not found on your profile. Sign in again and retry.
errors.missingMainKey: Unable to retrieve your encryption key. Sign in again and retry.
errors.rulesNotMet: The password does not meet all the rules.
errors.tooWeak: Password too easy to guess — try something more complex.

marketing.p1: Your password protects the key that encrypts your data. Changing it re-encrypts the key locally — your data stays intact.
marketing.p2: The server never sees the old or the new password: everything happens on your device before sending.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: The page you are looking for does not exist anymore.

form.title: Recover with a code
form.subtitle: Enter your email, your 12 recovery words, and a new password. Your data stays untouched.
form.mnemonicPlaceholder: Separate the words with spaces or hyphens — case doesn't matter.
form.confirmMismatch: The two passwords do not match.

password.title: Choose a new password
password.subtitle: Your code is verified. We’ll re-encrypt your key under this new password without touching your existing entries.

verify.title: Verify your code
verify.subtitle: First we confirm your code is valid. You’ll choose your new password in the next step.

passwordProof.instructionBefore: Confirm with your password to switch to mode

securityMode.headline: How many factors at each login.
securityMode.title: Security mode
securityMode.subtitle: Choose how many factors are required at each login. A change requires your password.

marketing.p1: The security mode governs what you must provide at each login: password alone, password + TOTP, or all three factors (password + passkey + TOTP).
marketing.p2: The stricter the requirement, the more you protect access — at the cost of an extra step at login. Your data is already encrypted client-side: the mode does not change the crypto, only the proofs the server asks for.

standard.label: Standard
standard.description: Password OR passkey is enough to sign in. The default.

always2fa.label: 2FA required
always2fa.description: A 2nd factor on top at each login: TOTP code or passkey, your choice. Set up at least one of the two to be able to select it.

maximum.label: Maximum
maximum.description: Password + passkey + TOTP, all three. A PRF-capable passkey is mandatory.

unmet.secondFactor: Enable TOTP or enroll a passkey first.

marketing.headline: Start fresh.
marketing.body1: Your password is also the key that encrypts your entries. Resetting it wipes your account clean — all existing data is deleted.
marketing.body2: This is by design: without the key, no one can recover the content — not even the Nodea team.

done.title: Password reset.
done.body: You can sign back in with your new password. The account has been wiped clean — your previous entries have been deleted.

invalidLink.title: This link is incomplete.
invalidLink.subtitle: Request a new email from the “forgot password” page.

form.title: Choose a new password.

warning.title: Permanent data loss
warning.body: The key that encrypts your entries is derived from your password. Since the old key was lost along with the old password, all your existing entries will be deleted when you confirm.

form.acknowledge: I understand that all my existing data will be deleted by this reset.

marketing.headline: Recover access.
marketing.body: Your password is also the key that encrypts your entries. Resetting it erases your existing data.

fork.title: Forgot password
fork.subtitle: Do you have a recovery code?

destroy.title: Reset without a code
destroy.subtitle: Enter your email — we’ll send you a link to set a new password.
destroy.warningBody: Encryption can’t be reversed without your original password.

sent.title: Check your inbox
sent.subtitleBefore: If a Nodea account is linked to
sent.subtitleAfter: , an email with a reset link has just been sent. The link is valid for 1 hour.
sent.warningTitle: The link will erase all your data
sent.warningBody: Only confirm if you accept a full reset.

layout.headline: One last step.
layout.marketing1: Your password alone isn’t enough in your security mode. Confirm with your TOTP or your passkey to finish opening your session.
layout.marketing2: If you no longer have access to your factor, you can request recovery by email — the 7-day delay protects you from malicious requests.

errors.sessionExpired: Session expired. Please sign in again.
errors.bypassAlreadyActive: A recovery request is already in progress.
errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
errors.tooManyAttempts: Too many attempts. Try again in a few minutes.
errors.wrongTotpCode: Wrong code. Try again with the current one.

picker.title: Choose your 2nd factor
picker.subtitle: Your security mode accepts two options. Use whichever is at hand — either one completes the sign-in.

totp.subtitleCode: Enter the code shown by your authenticator app (Bitwarden, Ente Auth, Aegis, Google Auth…). The code changes every 30 seconds.
totp.subtitleBackup: Enter one of your 10 backup codes (24 characters, dashes optional). Each code can only be used once.
totp.switchToBackup: I lost my TOTP → use a backup code

passkey.title: Confirm with your passkey
passkey.subtitle: Your security mode requires a passkey in addition to the TOTP code. Confirm with Touch ID, Face ID, Windows Hello or your hardware key to finalize the sign-in.
passkey.lostPasskey: I lost my passkey → request recovery by email

lost.sideEffectTotp: Your TOTP will be disabled and your backup codes invalidated.
lost.sideEffectPasskey: All your passkeys will be deleted — you can re-enroll them after signing in.
lost.confirmSubtitle: We’ll send you an email with a link to confirm. 7 days after your confirmation, your next sign-in will be allowed without {factor}. {sideEffect}
lost.sentSubtitle: Check your inbox. Confirm via the link — 7 days after that confirmation, you’ll be able to sign in again without {factor}.

marketing.headline: One more six-digit code.
marketing.paragraph1: TOTP (time-based one-time password) adds a second layer: at each login, your authenticator app (Bitwarden, Ente Auth, Aegis, Google Auth…) generates a 6-digit code valid for 30 seconds.
marketing.paragraph2: A leaked password is then no longer enough to get in — you also need your phone or your hardware key on hand.

enabled.intro: TOTP enabled. You’ll need a 6-digit code generated by your authenticator app at each login.
enabled.noneLeft: No codes left. Regenerate them now.

disabledActivate.intro: No TOTP configured. Type your password to generate the key and start the setup.

codes.title: Backup codes
codes.alertBody: TOTP is enabled. These 10 codes will help you out if you lose access to your authenticator app. Single-use, they’ll never be shown again.
codes.ackLabel: I’ve saved the 10 codes somewhere safe.

disable.title: Disable TOTP
disable.body: Confirming disables your TOTP. In maximum mode, or with 2FA required and no passkey enrolled, the mode automatically drops back to Standard; otherwise the passkey keeps covering the 2nd factor.

regen.title: Regenerate backup codes
regen.body: Type your password. The previous batch of codes will be invalidated immediately — make sure you can write down the new one.

display.title: New backup codes
display.alertBody: These 10 codes will never be shown again. The previous batch is invalidated.

secret.title: Scan the QR code
secret.subtitle: Open your authenticator app and add this account. If you can’t scan, reveal the key and type it in manually.

errors.invalidFormat: Type the 6 digits shown by your app.
errors.wrongCode: Wrong code. Try again with the current one.
errors.noEnrollment: No setup in progress. Start over from the beginning.

marketing.headline: A passkey instead of a password.
marketing.para1: A passkey is your phone’s fingerprint, your hardware key’s PIN, or your password manager. Confirming a sign-in becomes a single gesture — no more password to remember.
marketing.para2: A PRF-capable passkey (Touch ID, Face ID, Bitwarden, 1Password) can also decrypt your data — otherwise it signs you in but will still ask for your password to open your entries.

list.title: My passkeys
list.empty: No passkey registered yet. Add one to sign in without retyping your password.
list.subtitle: {count} · {prf}{note}.

add.title: Add a passkey
add.subtitle: Give it a name, retype your password, then confirm with your fingerprint / Face ID / PIN.

remove.title: Remove a passkey
remove.subtitleAfter: . This passkey will no longer be usable to sign in to your account.

pending.title: Activating…
pending.body: We're checking your link, one moment.

success.title: Account activated!
success.body: is ready. Redirecting to sign in…

invalid_token.title: Invalid link
invalid_token.body: This activation link is not recognized. It may have been truncated while copying. Start the sign-up again to get a new one.

already_consumed.title: Account already activated
already_consumed.body: This link has already been used. You can sign in directly.

expired.title: Link expired
expired.body: The activation link is valid for 7 days. Start the sign-up again to get a new one.

network.title: Network error
network.body: We couldn't reach the server. Check your connection and try again.

recoveryCode.headline: A safety net, with no backdoor.

marketing.p1: The recovery code is a backup password, in the form of 12 simple words to write down. It derives a key that encrypts the same main key as your password.
marketing.p2: If you forget your password, these 12 words let you recover your account without losing your data. Without them, the only option is to erase everything.

form.subtitleSetup: We'll generate 12 words to write down. Enter your password to authorize the generation.
form.subtitleRegenerate: Generate a new 12-word code. The old one will be invalidated immediately — make sure you can write down the new one before continuing.

recoveryDisplay.warningBody: These 12 words will never be shown to you again. Copy them on paper or in a password manager — without them, forgetting your password means losing all your data.
recoveryDisplay.verifyIntro: To confirm, re-type the requested words. They're hidden — keep your written list handy.
recoveryDisplay.verifyError: One or more words don't match. Check your list and try again.

marketing.p1: Someone asked to sign in without one of your 2FA factors (TOTP or passkey). You, hopefully.
marketing.p2: Once confirmed, the 7-day timer starts. This delay gives you time to react if you weren't the one who triggered the request — simply signing back in to Nodea as usual cancels it.
marketing.p3: On the next login after the delay, the factor will be removed and you'll be able to sign in again.

pending.title: Validating…
pending.body: We're checking your recovery link, one moment.

cancelled.title: Request cancelled
cancelled.body: This recovery request has already been cancelled. If you want to recover your lost factor again, start a new request from the sign-in page.

consumed.title: Request already applied
consumed.body: This request was applied on a previous login. Your factor has already been removed; just sign in as usual.

expired.title: Request expired
expired.body: This request has expired (links are valid for 7 days). Start a new request from the sign-in page.

unknown.title: Invalid link
unknown.body: This confirmation link is not recognized. It may have been truncated while copying. Start a new request from the sign-in page.

network.title: Network error
network.body: We couldn't reach the server. Check your connection and try again.

success.sideEffectTotp: Your TOTP will be disabled and your backup codes invalidated.
success.sideEffectPasskey: All your passkeys will be removed — you can re-enroll afterwards.
success.title: Request validated
success.subtitle: You'll be able to sign in without {factor} in 7 days. {sideEffect}
success.subtitleAlreadyConfirmed: You'd already clicked the link — the 7-day timer has been running since then. You'll be able to sign in without {factor} as soon as the timer reaches zero.
success.warningNotYouBefore: If you weren't the one who triggered this request, close this tab and
success.warningNotYouStrong: sign back in to Nodea as usual
success.warningNotYouAfter: . A successful login automatically cancels the bypass; no one will be able to bypass your {factor}.
success.warningCompromised: If you suspect your account is compromised, change your password from Account → Security.

countdown.expiredBody: You can sign in right now — the bypass will be applied on the next login.

deleteAccount.headline: A deletion with no way back.

marketing.p1: Deleting the account erases all your encrypted entries, your sessions and your invitations. No recovery is possible — remember to export first.
marketing.p2: Three confirmations: retype your email, your password, then one final confirmation.

dataExport.headline: Your data, in the clear, with you.
dataExport.marketing1: The export gathers all your decrypted entries into a single JSON file, generated entirely in your browser. Nothing passes through the server.
dataExport.marketing2: Since it outputs everything in the clear, we ask for your password again: fresh proof that it really is you.
dataExport.subtitle: Enter your password to authorise the export.

backup.headline: A backup that outlives the account.
backup.marketing1Before: The encrypted backup is a portable
backup.marketing1After: file, protected by a generated 12-word phrase — independent of your account, re-importable elsewhere.
backup.marketing2: First your password (proving it's you), then Nodea generates the 12-word phrase that encrypts the backup. That phrase has nothing to do with your password: it's what makes the file independent.
backup.subtitle: Enter your password to continue.
